decision: Use JWT access tokens with short expiry for API authentication
rationale: Stateless authentication avoids session storage and scales across API instances

decision: Store refresh tokens in httpOnly cookies, never in localStorage
rationale: httpOnly cookies keep refresh tokens out of reach of XSS attacks

decision: Hash passwords with argon2id instead of bcrypt
rationale: argon2id is memory-hard and the current OWASP recommendation for password hashing

decision: Authenticate service-to-service calls with bearer keys checked in auth middleware
rationale: Single bearer middleware keeps authentication checks consistent across every route

decision: Use OAuth device flow for CLI login instead of pasting API keys
rationale: Device flow login avoids long-lived keys sitting in shell history

decision: Use Postgres with pgvector for embedding storage and vector search
rationale: pgvector keeps vector search in the same Postgres database as relational data

decision: Write schema migrations as plain SQL files, no ORM migration DSL
rationale: Plain SQL migrations are reviewable and avoid ORM lock-in

decision: Use a single pg connection pool shared across the app
rationale: One shared pool avoids exhausting Postgres connections under load

decision: Query the database with raw parameterized SQL instead of an ORM
rationale: Parameterized SQL queries stay transparent and avoid ORM query surprises

decision: Index embedding columns with HNSW for approximate vector search
rationale: HNSW index makes nearest-neighbour vector search fast enough at our corpus size

decision: Style components with Tailwind utility classes, no CSS modules
rationale: Tailwind utility classes keep styling colocated with markup

decision: Manage client state with Zustand instead of Redux
rationale: Zustand keeps client state simple without Redux boilerplate

decision: Fetch server data with TanStack Query and cache by route
rationale: TanStack Query handles caching and refetching of server data declaratively

decision: Build UI components as small composable functions, one component per file
rationale: One component per file keeps the component tree easy to navigate

decision: Run tests with the node:test runner, assertions from node:assert/strict
rationale: node:test needs no test framework dependency and runs under bun test too

decision: Keep unit tests colocated with source files as *.test.ts
rationale: Colocated test files make missing test coverage obvious in review

decision: Test pure functions directly, mock nothing but the network boundary
rationale: Extracting pure functions beats mocking; mock only fetch at the network boundary

decision: Use deterministic fixtures for retrieval tests, no live embedding calls
rationale: Deterministic fixtures keep retrieval tests reproducible offline

decision: Ship Perception as a Docker image via docker compose
rationale: Docker compose gives self-hosted users one command to start Postgres and Perception

decision: Run CI on GitHub Actions with a single workflow for typecheck and tests
rationale: One GitHub Actions workflow keeps CI fast and easy to read

decision: Load environment variables from .env with shell env taking precedence
rationale: Shell environment wins over .env so deploys can override without editing files

decision: Pin the pnpm version in package.json packageManager field
rationale: Pinned pnpm version keeps installs reproducible across machines and CI

decision: Log structured JSON lines to stdout, no log files
rationale: Structured JSON logs on stdout let any collector parse them

decision: Return typed error responses with a stable error code field
rationale: Stable error codes let clients branch on failures without parsing messages
